title: Unlicensed S3 E3: Pest Control
summary: Unlicensed, the first fiction show created by Jeffrey and Joseph since Night Vale, returns for a third season.  Lou Rosen and Molly Hatch are unlicensed private detectives, scraping by on whatever cases they can get from their little office on the fringes of LA County. Reeling from a devastating betrayal and a major earthquake, this season finds Lou and Molly trying to rebuild their lives and evaluate their futures. But those futures might not be as long as they hope. Lou and Molly start what appears to be a quiet case: a series of strange home break-ins all involving ex-employees of a defunct Long Beach stereo manufacturer. But this case will quickly bring them up against an opponent more ruthless and resourceful than they've ever faced before. Lou and Molly have always gotten by on their wits, but what to do when facing an enemy who can outmatch them in both wits and violence? A cat and mouse game through the forgotten regions of Southern California, including a shady direct-to-video movie studio, the unglamorous workers neighborhoods of glamorous Catalina Island, and the distant remote parts of the desert, where help cannot reach you in time. There will be grave danger. There will be deaths. But maybe some things matter more than survival. Listen with a free Audible trial today.

title: As Time Goes By
summary: This Week On Sliding Through Dimensions: "As Time Goes By" (Season 2 Episode 13)  In this episode of Sliding Through Dimensions, we explore the season 2 finale of the seires "As Time Goes By." The Sliders find themselves repeatedly encountering the same individuals across three different worlds, notably Quinn's high school sweetheart, Daelin Richards.  On the first world, California is part of a spanish-ruled nation called Nueva Espana. Quinn reunites with Daelin, who is engaged to Dennis McMillan. When the Sliders are detained by immigration authorities, Quinn escapes and, with Daelin's help orchestrates a rescue. However, Dennis betrays them, leading to a tragic outcome.  Sliding into the next world, Quinn discovers Daelin is married to Dennis, who is abusive. After a confrontation, Quinn encourages Daelin to seek a better life, she decides to leave Dennis, taking thier child with her.  The final world presents a unique challenge: time flows backward. The Sliders are accused of Daelin's murder, but as events unfold in reverse, they must navigate this perplexing situation to clear there names and continue their journey.  Join us as we delve into the emotional and temporal complexities of "As Time Goes By," examining themes of love, destiny, and the consequences of choice across parallel worlds.     Sliding Through Dimensions YouTube Channel  www.youtube.com/@slidingthroughdimensions     Sliding Through Dimensions Twitch Channel  https://www.twitch.tv/slidingthroughdimensions     Feel free to send us any questions or comments to our email Slidingthroughdimensions@gmail.com and don't forget to like and subscribe to the podcast.     Follow Us:  Instagram: www.instagram.com/slidingthroughdimensions  Twitter: https://x.com/Slidinghome1   TikTok: www.tiktok.com/@slidingthroughdimensions      "In Till Next Time See You In The Next World"

title: Unlicensed S3 E1: Tiny Homes
summary: Unlicensed, the first fiction show created by Jeffrey and Joseph since Night Vale, returns for a third season.  Lou Rosen and Molly Hatch are unlicensed private detectives, scraping by on whatever cases they can get from their little office on the fringes of LA County. Reeling from a devastating betrayal and a major earthquake, this season finds Lou and Molly trying to rebuild their lives and evaluate their futures. But those futures might not be as long as they hope. Lou and Molly start what appears to be a quiet case: a series of strange home break-ins all involving ex-employees of a defunct Long Beach stereo manufacturer. But this case will quickly bring them up against an opponent more ruthless and resourceful than they've ever faced before. Lou and Molly have always gotten by on their wits, but what to do when facing an enemy who can outmatch them in both wits and violence? A cat and mouse game through the forgotten regions of Southern California, including a shady direct-to-video movie studio, the unglamorous workers neighborhoods of glamorous Catalina Island, and the distant remote parts of the desert, where help cannot reach you in time. There will be grave danger. There will be deaths. But maybe some things matter more than survival. Listen with a free Audible trial today.

summary: The Shisti and the Assistant Shisti of Mars chose Chicago, U.S.A., for their vacation spot. No worries; they were invisible. Plenty of rich food; the joint was loaded. This episode marks the first time James R. Adams has appeared on our podcast. We’d love to share more about him—but truth be told, there isn’t much known. He published five stories in the 1940s and the one you’re about to hear in the 1950s. That’s all we have… but sometimes, the stories speak for themselves. The last story in Planet Stories magazine in May 1953 can be found on page 104, Con-Fen by James R. Adams… Next on The Lost Sci-Fi Podcast, If werewolves exist, they don't necessarily conform to all the superstitions people have. They may even know fear….
